ps140: O venerable father Theodore, you offered yourself as a choice gift to God,* having acquired all-night vigilance and compunction,* divine instruction, utter humility, hope, faith and undiluted love,* watchfulness and loving-kindness, true dispassion and prayer,* which showed you to be a most radiant pillar of light.

O venerable father Theodore,* seeking with pious mind to clothe yourself in the vesture of truth* and the raiment of salvation,* you never ceased to cover your body with rags of sackcloth;* and thus, strengthened by the power of the divine Spirit,* you laid bare the wiles of him who stripped us naked.

Being poor in spirit, you brought yourself as a living sacrifice,* an acceptable whole-burnt offering* and a precious gift, O most blessed one,* to God who assumed our flesh from the Virgin Mother and beggared Himself for our sake.* Wherefore, you have received heavenly delight.* Pray to Him, we beseech you,* that He have mercy upon us.
theotokion: The pre-eternal God, receiving flesh through your blood,* showed you to be an intercessor for men, O pure one.* Wherefore, deliver your servants from all misfortune and every evil circumstance,* and from the snares of the wicked enemy,* and vouchsafe that all who glorify and do you homage* may share in the splendour of the elect.
stavrotheotokion: Beholding You crucified upon the Cross, O Jesus, accepting suffering willingly,* Your Virgin Mother cried out:* “Woe is me, O Master, my sweet Child!* How is it that You unjustly endured wounds,* O Physician who has healed the weakness of men* and delivered all from corruption by Your loving-kindness?"
